title: Chirality Of A Weyl Node
summary: The chirality of a Weyl node is the sign of the Jacobian relating momentum deviations to the Pauli-vector coefficients in the local Hamiltonian, equivalently the sign of its Berry-flux charge.
assumptions: The local crossing is simple and linear.
scope: Lecture One chirality sign from both local Hamiltonian and Berry-flux viewpoints.

source_anchors.notes: The sign of the determinant is the local chirality label.
Berry flux gives the same topological sign.

representation: Orientation sign of the local Weyl map.
failure_modes: Mixed sign conventions can flip the reported chirality without changing the physics.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks how chirality is computed or interpreted.
